skill.name: app-store-screenshots
skill.description: Use when building App Store screenshot pages, generating exportable marketing screenshots for iOS apps, or creating programmatic screenshot generators with Next.js. Triggers on app store, screenshots, marketing assets, html-to-image, phone mockup.
skill.summary: Generate professional App Store marketing screenshots with Next.js
skill.author: ParthJadhav

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 75 potential issues across 335 lines, all of which are false positives upon review. The findings consist of bash command examples in markdown code blocks (instructional documentation), legitimate use of html-to-image library for screenshot generation (core functionality), and MD5 hashes used for content integrity verification. No executable code, no network exfiltration, no credential access, and no malicious intent detected. This is a legitimate skill for generating App Store marketing assets.

content.user_title: Generate App Store Screenshots with Claude
content.value_statement: Creating professional App Store screenshots is tedious and time-consuming. This skill generates marketing-optimized iOS screenshots at Apple's required resolutions using Next.js and html-to-image.
actual_capabilities: Generate responsive Next.js pages for App Store screenshot creation
Export screenshots at Apple's required iPhone resolutions (6.1" to 6.9")
Apply marketing-first design principles to screenshot layouts
Create phone mockups with pre-measured iPhone frames
Handle typography scaling for high-resolution export
Implement double-call export trick for clean image output
limitations: Requires Next.js with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
User must provide their own app screenshots and icon
Export requires browser html-to-image library support
Designed for static screenshot generation, not interactive previews

use_cases.title: iOS Developer Launching New App
use_cases.description: An indie developer preparing their first app launch needs professional App Store screenshots that convert. They have basic screenshots but lack design expertise.

use_cases.title: Marketing Team Refreshing App Assets
use_cases.description: A product marketing team wants to refresh their App Store presence for a major version update. They need consistent, branded screenshots across all feature highlights.

use_cases.title: Agency Client Deliverables
use_cases.description: A design agency building an iOS app for a client needs to deliver App Store screenshots as part of the handoff. They require programmatic generation for consistency.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Screenshot Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate App Store screenshots for my coffee tracking app. I have screenshots in public/screenshots/, my icon is public/app-icon.png, brand colors are #8B4513 and #F5E6D3, I use Inter font, and I want 6 screenshots in a clean minimal style.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start with all required information provided upfront

prompt_templates.title: Feature-Focused Screenshots
prompt_templates.prompt: Create 8 App Store screenshots for my fitness app. Features: workout tracking, nutrition logging, progress charts, social challenges, Apple Watch sync, custom exercises, streak tracking, and premium insights. Style: bold and colorful with gradients.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides feature list and style direction, skill determines layout

prompt_templates.title: Brand-Guided Design
prompt_templates.prompt: I need 10 screenshots for my meditation app. Brand: warm organic style with soft gradients (purple to teal). Font: Calibri. Key differentiator is personalized recommendations. Include the Apple Watch app in one screenshot.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides specific brand direction and competitive positioning

prompt_templates.title: Copy-First Iteration
prompt_templates.prompt: Write headlines for my habit tracker app screenshots. Features: streaks, reminders, stats, categories, widgets, and dark mode. Target audience: people who want to build better daily routines. Give me 3 options per slide.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to refine copy before committing to layouts

output_examples.input: Generate 5 screenshots for my recipe app
output_examples.output:
I'll help you create App Store screenshots. First, I need some information:

1. Where are your app screenshots? (PNG files of actual device captures)
2. Where is your app icon PNG?
3. What are your brand colors?
4. What font does your app use?
5. What are your app's main features in priority order?
6. What style do you want? (warm/organic, dark/moody, clean/minimal, bold/colorful, etc.)

output_examples.input: Create screenshots with features: photo editor, filters, presets, batch export, raw support, and AI enhancement
output_examples.output:
Here are headline options for each screenshot:

**Slide 1 (Hero)**
- Paint a moment: "Edit any photo in seconds."
- State an outcome: "Your best photos, faster."
- Kill a pain: "Never settle for dull photos."

**Slide 2 (Differentiator)**
- Paint a moment: "AI that understands your style."
- State an outcome: "Edits that match your taste."
- Kill a pain: "Stop adjusting every single photo."

[continues for all 6 features...]

output_examples.input: Set up the project with bun
output_examples.output:
I'll set up the Next.js project with the required dependencies.

**Steps:**
1. Creating Next.js app with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
2. Installing html-to-image for screenshot export
3. Copying mockup.png to public/
4. Setting up font configuration in layout.tsx
5. Creating the screenshot generator page

**Project structure:**
- public/mockup.png (iPhone frame)
- public/app-icon.png (your icon)
- public/screenshots/ (your app screenshots)
- src/app/page.tsx (generator page)

best_practices: Answer all required questions (app screenshots, icon, brand colors, font, features, slide count, style) before starting code generation
Review and approve headline copy before layouts are built - bad copy ruins good design
Provide App Store screenshot references if you have a specific style in mind
Use high-resolution PNG screenshots from actual devices for best quality
Test the generated page and export functionality before finalizing designs
anti_patterns: Asking the skill to proceed without providing your app screenshots and icon
Requesting multiple features per screenshot - each slide should focus on one idea
Using complex jargon or compound phrases in headlines - keep them simple and punchy
Expecting the skill to create actual app screenshots - it only generates the marketing layouts

faq.question: What file formats do my app screenshots need?
faq.answer: Provide your app screenshots as PNG files captured from actual iOS devices. The skill uses these images inside the phone mockup frames.

faq.question: Can I use this for Android screenshots too?
faq.answer: This skill is designed specifically for iOS App Store requirements (iPhone portrait mode). Android screenshots have different size requirements and would need modifications.

faq.question: Do I need design experience to use this skill?
faq.answer: No design experience is needed. The skill applies marketing-first design principles automatically. You just need to provide your app assets and brand preferences.

faq.question: What if I don't have floating UI elements for decoration?
faq.answer: That's perfectly fine. The skill will focus on clean phone + caption layouts. Decorative elements are optional and only used if you provide them.

faq.question: Can I edit the generated screenshots later?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill creates a Next.js page you can modify. Adjust copy, colors, layouts, then re-export the screenshots at any time.

faq.question: Why does the export use a double-call trick?
faq.answer: The first html-to-image call forces lazy-loaded fonts and images to render. The second call produces clean output. Without this, exported images appear blank or missing assets.
